app.name: InnerTable
app.tagline: Get food recs from your trusted inner circle and pull a seat up to your inner table.
app.vision: In 3 years, Inner Table will be the category-defining recommendation app for friend groups who believe the best reviews come from people who know you.
app.mission: We believe the best restaurant recommendation isn't from an algorithm or a stranger ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ it's from a friend who knows you. Inner Table is where your inner circle keeps its list: the places worth going, the ones worth skipping, and the ones you can't wait to try together.

items.title: Edit/update comments
items.description:
Current Behaviour: Once comments are saved, they cannot be changed even by the original commenter.

Expected Behaviour: Comments should be editable or delete-able by the original commenter.  "Comments deleted" should be shown for deleted entries so that subsequent replies would still show in the correct order.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Comments can be edited within the app by the original commenter.
- Comment can be deleted within the app by the original commenter.
- Comments cannot be edited or delted by someone who did not make the comment.

items.title: Remove the filter by user
items.description:
Expected Behaviour: The by: filters should only allow filtering for Everyone or Just Mine.

Current Behaviour: There are additional unnecessary filters by users that are not needed
items.acceptanceCriteria: - There are only 2 options for By: filters.  Everyone, Just Mine.

items.title: Remove duplicate "Add Place" options on Landing Page
items.description:
Expected Behaviour: the landing page should not have the Add Place button on the bottom right.  The order of the buttons should be "View Restaurants", "View Bars", "Add a New Place"

Current Behaviour: There are two buttons that do the same thing (Add a New Place).  The bottom right one on the landing page should be removed.
items.acceptanceCriteria: Landing page does not have the bottom right button.
items.reproSteps:
1. Open the page or refresh the page to get to the landing page (type in user if needed)
2. View the landing page and see the redundancy of the add button.

items.title: Allow Reactions on comments

items.title: Support link to Maps

items.acceptanceCriteria: Link is available on the location of the entry in both list view and map view.

items.title: Trust-weighting
items.description: User connections should be weighted so that their recommendations have more certainty of good matches.  This should be done at different levels (e.g. Tony is more trusted for casual food but less for desserts.  Robert is more trusted for beer but less trusted for cocktails.  etc.)
items.acceptanceCriteria: More ideation needed

items.title: Per-User Status System
items.description:
Implement independent per-user statuses for restaurant entries so each user can have their own relationship with a place without affecting other users' views.

**Changes:**
- Add three entry states: Want to Go, Been + Recommend, Been + Skip (replaces the current two-option flow)
- Store Google Place ID at save time to enable duplicate detection
- When adding a place that already exists, prompt the user to attach their status to the existing entry instead of creating a duplicate
- Add `userStatuses` field per entry (per-user, like existing `userRatings`) to track each user's status independently
- Show recommend and skip counts on each card (e.g. ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ 2  ÃÂÃÂ°ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ 1), tappable to see names in a detail view
- Add "Hide my skips" filter to exclude places the current user has personally marked as skip
- Lightweight attach flow for non-authors: just status selection + star ratings, no need to re-enter place details

**Out of scope for this release:** notifications, trust-weighted filtering, visit history

items.title: My Skips bugs
items.description:
Users need the ability to mark entries as "Go now!" or "Skip it!" to support the ability to filter out the places that a user has marked as a skip or recommend.

We'll redesign the whole workflow.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
# Prompt: Redesign the "Add a Place" Workflow in index.html

## Context
This is a single-file web app (`index.html`) with inline CSS and JavaScript. It uses Firebase Realtime Database and the Google Maps Places API. I want to redesign the "Add a Place" modal flow. Please read the full file carefully before making changes.

---

## Current Behavior (what to replace)

When the user clicks "Add a Place" (the FAB button or the home CTA), `openModal()` is called. It opens a modal that shows **Step 1: a type chooser** with three cards:
- "Want to Go" ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ calls `selectAddType('want-to-go')`
- "Go Now" ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ calls `selectAddType('been-recommend')`
- "Been + Hard Pass" ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ calls `selectAddType('been-skip')`

After selecting one, the modal transitions to **Step 2: the form** (`#form-step`), with a "ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ Back" link to return to Step 1.

---

## New Behavior (what to build)

### Remove the two-step flow entirely.
Clicking "Add a Place" should go **directly** to a single-screen form ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ no pop-up type chooser. The `#type-step` div and all its type-card buttons can be removed. The `backToTypeStep()` function and the "ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ Back" step link in the form are no longer needed.

### New form layout (top to bottom):

1. **Name** field (keep the existing Google Places autocomplete ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ `#f-name` with `#places-dropdown`)
2. **Location / Neighborhood** field (`#f-location`, auto-filled by Places)
3. **Type** toggle: Restaurant / Bar (keep the existing `#tog-restaurant` / `#tog-bar` toggle group)
4. **Experience toggle**: A two-button toggle for **"I've Been"** vs **"Want to Try"**
   - This replaces the old type-card step as the primary branching mechanism
   - Style it like the existing `.rec-toggle` / `.rec-btn` pattern
   - Default to no selection (neither active) when the modal first opens
   - The rest of the form below this toggle dynamically shows/hides based on selection

5. **If "I've Been" is selected**, show (in this order):
   - A **single row** containing:
     - **Overall Rating** star picker (left side, existing `#star-picker` logic)
     - **Go Now! / Hard Pass** two-button toggle (right side, on the **same row**, side by side with the stars)
     - Style the Go Now button active state in olive/green (matching `.rec-btn.yes.active`) and Hard Pass in the existing muted/grey style (`.rec-btn.no.active`)
   - **Cuisine** dropdown (`#f-cuisine`) and **Price Range** dropdown (`#f-price`) ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ keep both, in the existing side-by-side `form-row` layout
   - **Detailed Ratings** section (Quality, Service, Value, Ambiance ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ keep as-is, labeled "Optional")
   - **Notes** / "Your take" textarea (`#f-notes`)
   - **Save** button

6. **If "Want to Try" is selected**, show:
   - **Note** textarea (`#f-try-note`, optional)
   - **Link / Review URL** field (`#f-url`, optional)
   - **Save** button

7. The **Save** button should remain disabled until:
   - For "I've Been": Name is filled in AND either Go Now or Hard Pass is selected
   - For "Want to Try": Name is filled in (notes/URL are optional)
   - If neither "I've Been" nor "Want to Try" is chosen yet, Save is disabled

---

## Logic / JS Changes

- `openModal()`: Skip showing `#type-step`. Show `#form-step` directly. Reset the new experience toggle (clear both "I've Been" / "Want to Try" active states). Hide both `#been-fields` and `#try-fields` until the user picks one.
- `selectAddType()`: Keep this function but call it from the new experience toggle buttons instead of the old type-cards. The "I've Been" button should call `selectAddType('been-recommend')` initially (setting `addType = 'been-recommend'`). When the user later picks Go Now or Hard Pass in the rating row, update `addType` accordingly (`'been-recommend'` or `'been-skip'`). The "Want to Try" button calls `selectAddType('want-to-go')`.
- The Go Now / Hard Pass toggle within the "I've Been" section should update the `addType` state variable:
  - Go Now selected ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ `addType = 'been-recommend'`
  - Hard Pass selected ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ `addType = 'been-skip'`
- The existing `submitRec()` function logic should continue to work ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ it already reads `addType` to determine the status saved to Firebase.
- Remove `backToTypeStep()` function (no longer needed).

---

## Attach Step Redesign

The `#attach-step` (where a non-author user adds their own status to an existing entry) should also be redesigned to match the new pattern:

- Remove the three type-cards (Want to Go / Go Now / Been + Hard Pass) from `#attach-step`
- Replace with the same **"I've Been" / "Want to Try"** toggle
- If "I've Been": show the rating section (`#attach-rating-section`) with the Go Now / Hard Pass toggle inline on the same row as the overall stars (same layout as the main form)
- If "Want to Try": hide the rating section, just show Save
- The existing `setAttachStatus()` function should be called from the new toggle buttons and the Go Now / Hard Pass toggle within it

---

## Icon Changes (Apply Everywhere in the App)

Replace the current "Go Now" and "Hard Pass" emoji/icons throughout the **entire app** ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ including cards, filter tabs, buttons, status tags, and any other UI location where these statuses appear.

### Hard Pass icon
Replace all `ÃÂÃÂ°ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ` instances used in the "Hard Pass" / "not-recommended" context with the **ÃÂÃÂ°ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ«** emoji (circle with a diagonal line / no-entry symbol).

### Go Now icon
The current `ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ` checkmark is used for "Go Now" / "recommended." Replace it with a **green box with a white checkmark**, rendered as a small inline CSS element (not an emoji). Implement it as a `<span>` styled with a green background (`#4a7c4a` or similar), white color, rounded corners, and a bold checkmark character (ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ) inside ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ sized to feel like an inline badge/icon. Use a consistent CSS class (e.g., `.go-icon`) so it can be reused wherever the icon appears throughout the app.

Make sure to update:
- `cardHTML()` function ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ the `statusTag` for `not-recommended` and `recommended`
- The `beenBtnLabel` logic in the non-author inline rating section
- The type-card icons in `#attach-step` (after redesigning to toggles, the Go Now / Hard Pass toggle button labels)
- Any filter tab labels or other status text that reference these icons
- The vote buttons (`ÃÂÃÂ°ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ` used for "Disagree" vote) ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ **do NOT change those**, as they are not status icons but voting buttons

---

## Style Notes

- Keep all existing CSS variables (`--rust`, `--olive`, `--slate`, etc.)
- The new "I've Been / Want to Try" toggle should use the existing `.rec-toggle` / `.rec-btn` CSS pattern
- The Go Now / Hard Pass row should use a flex layout: stars on the left (`flex: 1`), toggle group on the right ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ both vertically centered
- No new external dependencies ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ keep it as a single self-contained file

---

## What NOT to change

- Firebase config and data structure
- Google Maps / Places API autocomplete logic
- The duplicate detection / `#dup-overlay` flow
- The `editEntry()` flow (editing an existing entry)
- The `upgradeToTried()` flow
- Comment system, vote system, reactions
- The `#place-detail-overlay` panel
- The welcome screen and auth logic
- The list/map view, filter chips, and card rendering (except icon updates above)

items.title: View reactions
items.description:
Users should be able to click on the reaction emojis in the comments to see who has reacted and with what emoji.  

Also individual users should only be able to react once to each comment.  If a user has already reacted and clicks again, their reaction should be deleted.  The user can then re-click again if they want to react with the same or different emoji.

User reactions should be shown in aggregate per reaction (e.g. thumbs up icon with total thumbs up count, thumbs laughing icon with the total count of laughing reactions, etc)

There should be a separate button for users to add their reaction.  The behavior of that button is that a click of the button will bring up all available reaction emojis (no long click needed). Remove the applause emoji.  Add the thumbs down emoji (list it next to the thumbs up emoji)

items.title: [v0.2] Define users table and auth schema in Supabase
items.description:
CONTEXT: Supabase auth creates an auth.users row automatically. We need a public.users table that extends it with app-specific fields.

GOAL: Create the public.users table (and its RLS policies) that every other feature will build on.

SCHEMA:
- id uuid primary key references auth.users(id) on delete cascade
- display_name text not null
- handle text unique (for friend-finding later)
- avatar_url text
- created_at timestamptz default now()
- updated_at timestamptz default now()

RLS POLICIES:
- Anyone authenticated can SELECT any user row (needed for friend search later).
- Users can only UPDATE their own row.
- INSERT happens via a trigger on auth.users insert (auto-create public.users row with display_name from metadata).

Write the SQL as a Supabase migration in supabase/migrations/. Don't hand-execute in the dashboard.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- public.users table created via migration file.
- Trigger auto-creates a public.users row when auth.users inserts.
- RLS policies match spec; test by creating a throwaway auth user and confirming visibility.
- Migration is idempotent (rerunnable).

items.title: [v0.2] Replace nickname flow with Supabase Google sign-in
items.description:
CONTEXT: Today src/js/auth.js stores a nickname in localStorage as the 'user'. We need real auth.

GOAL: Replace the welcome-screen nickname prompt with a Google sign-in button powered by Supabase Auth. Keep the welcome card visual styling.

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add @supabase/supabase-js via CDN (or import map).
- Replace the current name-input + Enter-app flow with a 'Sign in with Google' button that calls supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'google' }).
- On auth state change, populate currentUser with the Supabase user (id, display_name from metadata).
- Rename currentUser (which is a string today) to a richer object { id, display_name, avatar_url }.
- Add a sign-out option in the header user-chip menu.

CONSTRAINTS: Do not yet wire Supabase to the recommendations data — we still read/write Firebase RTDB in this release. This item is ONLY the auth layer. Data migration is a separate item.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Welcome screen shows 'Sign in with Google' instead of a nickname input.
- After sign-in, header shows the Google display name + avatar.
- Sign-out option works and returns user to welcome screen.
- currentUser everywhere in the codebase now references .display_name / .id instead of being a raw string.
- App still reads/writes to Firebase RTDB as before (migration comes later).

items.title: [v0.2] Port recommendations schema from Firebase RTDB to Supabase (recommendations table v1)
items.description:
CONTEXT: We need the existing 'recommendation' object representable in Postgres so the next item can migrate data. We'll normalize further in v0.3 — for now, mirror the denormalized Firebase shape 1:1 in a single table.

GOAL: Create a recommendations table in Supabase that holds the same fields as today's Firebase entries, plus author_id referencing public.users.

SCHEMA (v0.2, denormalized — this gets split in v0.3):
- id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()
- author_id uuid references public.users(id) not null
- name text, place_type text, location text, lat double precision, lng double precision, google_place_id text
- status text (try / recommended / not-recommended), rating int, cuisine text, price text
- notes text, try_note text, url text
- factor_ratings jsonb (quality/service/value/ambiance)
- created_at timestamptz default now(), updated_at timestamptz

ALSO: comments, reactions, votes as separate tables (denormalized comment_reactions jsonb column is fine for v0.2).

RLS: authenticated users can read all rows; users can insert/update/delete only their own author_id rows.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- recommendations, comments, votes tables created via migrations.
- RLS policies block unauthorized writes (verify with a throwaway user).
- Migration files are idempotent.

items.title: [v0.2] One-time data migration script: Firebase RTDB -> Supabase
items.description:
CONTEXT: Move the existing prototype data out of Firebase into Supabase without losing history.

GOAL: Write a Node.js script (scripts/migrate-firebase-to-supabase.js) that reads every recommendation, comment, reaction, and vote from Firebase and writes them into Supabase.

KEY CHALLENGE: Today author, votes[user], userRatings[user], and comments[].author are all nickname STRINGS. We need to map nickname -> UUID. Approach:
- Enumerate all distinct nicknames from the Firebase export.
- For each, either (a) match to an existing auth user (if that person has already signed in via Google and their display_name matches) or (b) create a placeholder user row in public.users with a generated handle and no auth.users entry yet.
- When those people later sign in with Google, we'll merge their auth.users row with the placeholder by display_name or by a one-time linking UI.

Produce a mapping file migrations/nickname-uuid-map.json for audit.

Run in dry-run mode first; require --execute flag to actually write.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Script runs end-to-end in dry-run mode without errors.
- --execute mode writes all data to Supabase and produces nickname-uuid-map.json.
- Row counts match: #recs in Firebase == #recs in Supabase, same for comments/votes.
- Spot-check 3 specific entries to verify timestamps, ratings, and factor ratings preserved.

items.title: [v0.2] Replace Firebase RTDB calls with Supabase throughout the app
items.description:
CONTEXT: With auth and data in Supabase, cut over all reads and writes.

GOAL: Replace every firebase.database() call in src/js/ with an equivalent Supabase client call.

FILES WITH FIREBASE REFS: src/js/firebase.js (delete), src/js/places-service.js (most references — loadRecs, submitRec, castVote, comment CRUD, reactions), src/js/app.js (reaction updates), index.html (remove firebase script tags, add supabase).

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Preserve the live-update UX. Use Supabase Realtime (supabase.channel + .on('postgres_changes')) to replace db.ref().on('value').
- Keep the allRecs shape the same so ui-render.js doesn't need heavy changes. Adapter pattern: Supabase rows get reshaped into the current object structure at the boundary.
- Delete config.js firebaseConfig block entirely once nothing references it.

This is the biggest item in v0.2. Consider doing it branch-first, keeping Firebase live as a fallback, then cutting over.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- No firebase.* references remain in src/js/.
- App reads/writes to Supabase for all operations.
- Live-update (new recs/comments/reactions appearing in real time) still works.
- All existing features work: add place, rate, comment, react, vote, edit comment, delete comment.
- Smoke test: sign in as two users in different browsers, post/react, confirm both see updates.

items.title: [v0.2] Remove hardcoded admin password and move API keys to env vars
items.description:
CONTEXT: src/js/config.js has a hardcoded ADMIN_PASSWORD ('sactown') and API keys committed to the repo. Security risk flagged in a code comment already.

GOAL: Move every secret to env vars; define admin status as a column on the users table instead of a client-side password.

STEPS:
- Add is_admin boolean column to public.users, default false. Set your own row to true.
- Remove ADMIN_USER / ADMIN_PASSWORD from config.js.
- Replace the 'isAdmin' logic throughout the app with a check against currentUser.is_admin.
- Move GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY to an env var loaded at build/runtime.
- Rotate the previously-leaked Google Maps key in Google Cloud Console.
- Rotate the Firebase API key (even though we're leaving Firebase — hygiene).

If the static hosting setup doesn't support env var injection, use a small build step (even a shell script that sed's the key into a generated config.js at deploy time) — don't commit secrets.

items.title: [v0.3] Create normalized places table + backfill from recommendations
items.description:
CONTEXT: Today 'a place' is conflated with 'a user's experience of it' in the recommendations table. Each place gets re-entered by every user. We're splitting them.

GOAL: Create a places table as a first-class entity and backfill from existing recommendations, deduplicating by google_place_id (fall back to normalized name+location if placeId missing).

SCHEMA:
- id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()
- google_place_id text unique
- name text not null
- location text, lat double precision, lng double precision
- place_type text (restaurant/bar), cuisine text, price text
- created_at timestamptz, created_by uuid references users(id)

BACKFILL SCRIPT: scripts/backfill-places.sql — groups recommendations by google_place_id, picks the earliest, inserts into places, records each resulting place.id back onto the recommendations rows in a new place_id column.
items.acceptanceCriteria:

- No orphan places (each place has at least one linked recommendation).
- Duplicate detection logic documented (prefer google_place_id; fallback rules specified).

SCHEMA:
- id uuid primary key, user_id uuid not null, place_id uuid not null
- unique (user_id, place_id)
- status text ('try' / 'been-recommend' / 'been-skip')
- overall_rating int check (0..5)

After migration, the old recommendations table can be renamed to recommendations_legacy and kept read-only for a release as a safety net, then dropped in v0.4.

items.title: [v0.3] Split comments and reactions into their own tables
items.description:

GOAL: Create comments and comment_reactions tables keyed to entries, and migrate existing comment data.

SCHEMA:

IMPLEMENTATION: Fetch entries joined to places and users. Group entries by place_id in JS. Render one card per place with a sub-list of takes.

items.title: [v0.4] Create friendships table with directional edges and status
items.description:
CONTEXT: Ego-network model. Friendships are directional with a mutual-accept flow. Alice requests Bob; Bob accepts; now both have accepted edges.

GOAL: Create friendships table and the state machine around it.

SCHEMA:
- user_id uuid, friend_id uuid (both reference users)
- status text ('pending', 'accepted', 'blocked', 'removed')
- primary key (user_id, friend_id)
- created_at, accepted_at timestamptz

SEMANTICS:
- Alice sending a request to Bob creates two rows: (alice->bob, status=pending) and (bob->alice, status=pending-incoming). Decide on convention and document it.
- On acceptance, both rows flip to 'accepted'.
- On block, only the blocker's outbound row flips to 'blocked'; the blocked party can still see their 'removed' row.

RLS: users can read their own friendship rows and ones where they're the target. Writes only via security-definer functions add_friend / accept_friend / remove_friend / block_user.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- friendships table + 4 SECURITY DEFINER RPC functions exist.
- Happy path works: alice invites bob, bob accepts, both rows accepted.
- Block works: blocker no longer sees the blocked user in friend search/feed.
- Rejected friendship requests are removed (or marked 'removed'), not silently deleted from audit perspective.

items.title: [v0.4] Friend search UI (by handle or email)

UI:

PRIVACY: email search returns a match only if the target user has enabled 'allow_email_lookup' in their profile settings (add this column now).
items.acceptanceCriteria:

- Send-request button triggers add_friend RPC.

items.title: [v0.4] Row-Level Security policies for circle-based visibility
items.description:
CONTEXT: By default, entries should be visible only to the author and their accepted friends. Users can opt in to broader visibility per entry.

GOAL: Add visibility column to entries and corresponding RLS policies.

SCHEMA:
- entries.visibility text check (visibility in ('private','friends','friends_of_friends','public')) default 'friends'

RLS POLICIES on entries for SELECT:
- visibility = 'private' → only author.
- visibility = 'friends' → author OR accepted friend (exists in friendships where user_id=author_id and friend_id=auth.uid() and status='accepted').
- visibility = 'friends_of_friends' → above OR a friend-of-a-friend (2-hop join).
- visibility = 'public' → any authenticated user.

PERFORMANCE: the FoF subquery needs an index. Consider a materialized view friendships_2hop that precomputes 2-hop edges per user, refreshed nightly; for v0.4, direct-query is fine if the graph is small.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Per-entry visibility enforced at the DB layer (not just UI).
- Test: create two users, befriend, confirm Alice sees Bob's friends-only entries; delete friendship, confirm she no longer sees them.
- FoF query works correctly on a 3-user test graph.

items.title: [v0.5] 'Rate my friends' taste' page — 5-dot casual grid
items.description:
CONTEXT: The casual user's surface for setting friend_taste_overrides. A grid of friends × categories, each cell a 5-dot control.

GOAL: Build a page where the user sees their accepted friends as rows and their most-used categories as columns. Each cell is a 5-dot button row that maps to similarity_score under the hood (1 dot = -1 to -0.5, 2 dots = -0.5 to 0, 3 dots = 0 ≈ neutral, 4 dots = 0 to 0.5, 5 dots = 0.5 to 1).

UX:

Minor release-polish item; not structural.

items.title: [v0.6] Create similarity_scores table + indexes
items.description:
CONTEXT: Storage for computed per-category user-to-user similarity.

GOAL: Create similarity_scores table populated by a nightly job.

SCHEMA:
- user_a uuid, user_b uuid, category_id uuid
- raw_similarity numeric (Pearson correlation, -1..1)
- shrunk_similarity numeric (after empirical Bayes shrinkage)
- n_shared int (overlap count)
- computed_at timestamptz
- primary key (user_a, user_b, category_id)

NOTE: store both (a,b) and (b,a) for lookup speed, OR enforce a < b and always look up both directions. Pick one; document. The symmetric-pair approach saves storage; the both-directions approach saves a query at read time. For v0.6 use symmetric-pair (a < b).

Indexes: (user_a, category_id), (user_b, category_id).
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Table + indexes created.
- Documented convention (symmetric pair).
- RLS: users can only read rows involving themselves.

items.title: [v0.6] Nightly Edge Function: compute per-category Pearson similarity
items.description:
CONTEXT: The core model: for each pair of users who've rated places in the same category, compute Pearson correlation on their overall_ratings.

GOAL: Write a Supabase Edge Function that runs nightly (via Supabase cron) and recomputes similarity_scores for all (user_a, user_b, category) triples with n_shared >= 2.

ALGORITHM:
- For each category_id, pull all entries joined to place_categories filtered to that category.
- For each pair of users with >= 2 shared places in that category, compute Pearson r.
- Store (user_a, user_b, category_id, raw_similarity=r, n_shared).

PERFORMANCE: batch updates via a single transaction per category. For a small userbase this is fine; for scale, revisit later with a window or delta-based approach.

Do NOT compute shrunk_similarity yet — that's the next item.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Edge function runs end-to-end on a 5-user, 50-entry test dataset.
- Row counts match expected (#pairs per category × #overlap threshold).
- Pearson output sanity-checked on a hand-constructed case.
- Scheduled via supabase cron (nightly).

items.title: [v0.6] Empirical Bayes shrinkage for low-overlap similarity
items.description:
CONTEXT: With small n_shared, Pearson r is noise. Shrink toward a neutral prior.

GOAL: Extend the compute_similarity function to populate shrunk_similarity.

FORMULA (James-Stein-ish):
- prior_mu = 0 (neutral), prior_tau = population variance of raw_similarity within the category
- observation_variance = (1 - raw^2)^2 / (n_shared - 2) if n_shared > 2 else large
- weight = prior_tau / (prior_tau + observation_variance)
- shrunk = weight * raw + (1 - weight) * prior_mu

Result: with 2 shared ratings, nearly all weight on prior (shrunk ≈ 0). With 50 shared, nearly all weight on raw.

Document the math in a short comment block so a curious user reading the code (or a quant user viewing raw/shrunk side-by-side) can follow it.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- shrunk_similarity populated for all similarity_scores rows.
- Hand-calc check: pair with n=2 has shrunk ≈ 0; pair with n=50 has shrunk ≈ raw.
- Comment block explains the formula.

items.title: [v0.6] Prediction engine: blend explicit override + implicit similarity → final trust weight
items.description:
CONTEXT: We now have two trust signals: friend_taste_overrides (explicit, user-set) and similarity_scores (implicit, computed). Blend them.

GOAL: Build a Postgres function or view (v_effective_trust) that returns, for a given rater and category, the effective trust weight for every other user.

BLEND RULE:
- If explicit override exists: effective = explicit_weight_bias * explicit + (1 - explicit_weight_bias) * shrunk_implicit
- explicit_weight_bias = min(1.0, n_explicit_overrides_by_rater / 20) → starts at 0 (trust implicit more), approaches 1 as user provides more overrides.
- If no explicit override and shrunk_implicit is non-null: effective = shrunk_implicit.
- If neither: effective = 0 (unknown).

Return columns: rater_id, friend_id, category_id, effective, source ('explicit' / 'implicit' / 'blended' / 'unknown'), precision.

The UI will read from this view to both render and explain weights.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- v_effective_trust view returns a row per (rater, friend, category) triple where we have any signal.
- source column correctly identifies the blend origin.
- Hand-verify on a test user who has both explicit and implicit signals.

items.title: [v0.7] Implement 2-hop and 3-hop trust propagation with decay
items.description:
CONTEXT: Extend the trust graph to friends-of-friends with attenuation.

GOAL: Extend v_effective_trust (or add v_effective_trust_multihop) to compute propagated trust to 2nd and 3rd-degree connections.

FORMULA: trust(A→C, cat) = max over paths A→…→C of (product of edge trusts × decay^hop_count).
- decay = 0.7 per hop (configurable).
- Cap at 3 hops.
- Keep the best (highest) path if multiple exist.

IMPLEMENTATION: use a recursive CTE over accepted friendships joined to v_effective_trust. Store results in a trust_edges_multihop materialized view, refreshed nightly alongside similarity_scores.

Watch out for graph explosion — limit by visiting a user once per path and cap branching factor.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Materialized view populated; queryable by (rater_id, target_id, category).
- On a 5-user test graph (A-B-C-D-E linear), verify A→C trust = T(A→B)*T(B→C)*0.7 and A→E = product * 0.7^3.
- Refresh runs nightly.

items.title: [v0.7] Enforce FoF privacy at query time
items.description:
CONTEXT: FoF visibility must not leak entries from users who set visibility='friends'.

GOAL: Audit predict_place and the Discover query to confirm entries with visibility='friends' cannot appear via a 2-hop path. Add tests.

Write a pgTAP or SQL test script that creates 3 users (A, B, C), friendships A-B and B-C, entries from C with each visibility level, and asserts A sees the FoF and Public entries from C but NOT the Friends-only entry.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- SQL test verifies privacy at all three visibility levels.
- Test passes.
- CI runs the test (optional — can be manual for now).

items.title: [v0.8] Create external_reviewers and external_reviewer_ratings tables
items.description:
CONTEXT: Add a third tier of rating source — professional/influencer reviewers.

GOAL: Create the reviewer tables.

SCHEMA:
- external_reviewers: id uuid, display_name, handle, platform ('instagram'/'youtube'/'website'/'publication'), avatar_url, bio, is_professional bool, created_at.
- external_reviewer_ratings: reviewer_id, place_id, rating numeric, rating_scale text ('0-5','0-10','1-100','michelin_stars'), category_id, source_url, review_excerpt, review_date, imported_at.
- reviewer_stats (materialized view): reviewer_id, category_id, mean_rating_normalized, std_rating, n_ratings.

RLS: reviewer tables are public-readable (authenticated users).
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- All three tables/views created.
- reviewer_stats refreshable via a scheduled job.

items.title: [v0.8] Admin import tool for reviewer data (CSV upload)
items.description:
CONTEXT: We need a way to load reviewer ratings without hand-writing SQL.

GOAL: Build a simple admin-only import page (gated by is_admin) that accepts CSV uploads and ingests them.

CSV SCHEMA: reviewer_handle, place_name, google_place_id (optional), rating, rating_scale, category_slug, source_url, review_excerpt, review_date.

RESOLUTION LOGIC:
- If google_place_id: lookup/upsert places.
- Else: fuzzy match on place_name + location; if no confident match, flag for review (dry-run output).

PRODUCE: a preview view before committing the import (show new vs existing, ambiguous matches).
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Admin can upload a CSV and see a preview.
- Confirming the preview inserts rows transactionally.
- Ambiguous place matches flagged for manual resolution.
- Non-admin users 404 on the page.

items.title: [v0.8] Seed 8-12 reviewers and 300-500 places for launch city
items.description:
CONTEXT: Content problem, not engineering. Need enough reviewer coverage for v0.9 to feel alive.

GOAL: Pick a launch city (probably SF given project context). Curate 8-12 reviewer/publication sources with structured ratings. Import their top 30-50 places each.

SUGGESTED SOURCES:
- The Infatuation SF (structured 0-10 ratings)
- Eater SF essential lists and Top Chef reviews
- SF Chronicle Top 100 Restaurants
- Michelin Guide SF (star count)
- A few high-coverage Instagram food accounts (Lucas Sin, Topjaw if he covers SF, plus 2-3 SF-specific accounts)
- Beli leaderboards (if licensable/scrapable)

LEGAL: attribute every rating with source_url. Don't reproduce full review text — excerpt only. If unclear, default to link-only.

Deliver a CSV + import run + summary of coverage: how many places we now have, median ratings per reviewer, category distribution.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- 300-500 places in the launch-city database with at least one reviewer rating.
- 8-12 reviewers seeded.
- Coverage report generated.
- All ratings attributed to source_url.

items.title: [v0.8] Create user_reviewer_trust table (parallel to friend_taste_overrides)
items.description:
CONTEXT: Users can explicitly rate their trust in reviewers, same pattern as for friends.

GOAL: Create user_reviewer_trust table.

SCHEMA:
- user_id, reviewer_id, category_id
- similarity_score numeric (0..1 mapped from dot scale)
- updated_at
- primary key (user_id, reviewer_id, category_id)
items.acceptanceCriteria: - Table + RLS created. Reused by the 'Rate your reviewers' UI.

items.title: [v0.8] 'Rate your reviewers' UI (grid view, dual casual/quant)
items.description:
CONTEXT: Mirrors the friend-taste grid from v0.5.

GOAL: New page: 'Reviewers I follow'. Lists the reviewers in the DB, lets the user tune per-category trust (5-dot casual / 0-1 quant).

Reuse the grid component from v0.5 (generalize it). Each reviewer row has avatar, handle, platform icon, and category dots.

Include a 'Follow / Unfollow' toggle — unfollowing sets all category dots to 0 and removes the reviewer from blending.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Page renders all reviewers with correct metadata.
- Dots write to user_reviewer_trust.
- Follow/Unfollow works.
- Both casual and quant views supported.

items.title: [v0.8] Swipe-based reviewer calibration game for onboarding
items.description:
CONTEXT: Rather than forcing new users to rate every reviewer on every category, collect implicit signal via swipes.

GOAL: Build a swipe UI: show 20 cards, each with a reviewer's pick (name, rating, excerpt) and a ✓/✗/meh choice. Each swipe writes an implicit signal used to seed user_reviewer_trust.

UX:
- Card: 'Lucas Sin rated Mister Jiu's 4.5★ — would you go?' ✓ ✗ meh
- Show the card's category (Chinese, etc.) to correctly attribute.
- On completion, compute per-reviewer-per-category agreement and populate user_reviewer_trust with initial 0.3-0.7 dots based on agreement rate.
- Skippable; re-entrable later as a 'Tune my reviewers' game.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Card-swipe UX works on mobile and desktop.
- 20-card session completes in under 2 minutes.
- Initial trust weights populated post-session.
- User can rerun the game from settings.

items.title: [v0.8] Extend predict_place to include reviewer tier (three tiers active)
items.description:
CONTEXT: The reviewer tier was stubbed in v0.6. Now populate it.

GOAL: Modify predict_place so the reviewers tier reads from external_reviewer_ratings joined to user_reviewer_trust, with the same precision-weighted aggregation used for circle.

BIAS CORRECTION: z-score reviewer ratings using reviewer_stats per category before blending into the prediction (raw ratings still surfaced in the UI for the tier display — users want to see Topjaw's actual 9/10, not a z-score).

RESULT: tiers.reviewers.{rating, count, contributors} now populated.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Places with reviewer ratings return a populated reviewers tier.
- Bias correction applied correctly (verify with a reviewer who rates consistently high).
- Three-tier display renders end-to-end.

items.title: [v0.9] Unified trust_edges table (users + reviewers + public aggregates)
items.description:
CONTEXT: Three separate trust-source paths (friends, reviewers, Google) is clean during build but adds complexity to the prediction engine. Unify.

GOAL: Create a unified trust_edges view that UNIONs:
- (observer_id, 'user', friend_id, category_id, weight, precision) from v_effective_trust_multihop
- (observer_id, 'reviewer', reviewer_id, category_id, weight, precision) from user_reviewer_trust
- (null, 'public', null, category_id, weight_floor, prior_precision) — a constant baseline row per category

Simplify predict_place to query this one view instead of three separate paths.

Keep the underlying tables unchanged — this is an abstraction layer, not a data migration.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Unified view works; predict_place refactored to use it.
- All three tiers still render correctly.
- Query plan is not worse than the pre-refactor version.

items.title: [v0.9] Spread-tag classifier (Consensus / Hidden Gem / Tourist Trap / etc.)
items.description:
CONTEXT: The flagship v0.9 feature. Auto-label every place based on the rating spread across tiers.

GOAL: Add logic to predict_place that computes a tag based on these rules:
- 'consensus': all available tiers within 0.3 stars of each other.
- 'hidden-gem': circle >= google + 0.5 (requires circle n >= 3).
- 'tourist-trap': circle <= google - 0.5 (requires circle n >= 3).
- 'critic-pick': reviewers >= google + 0.5 AND circle missing.
- 'divided': reviewers and circle differ by >= 0.7.
- 'unknown-to-circle': only google available.
- 'sparse-signal': total contributors < 3 even across tiers — flag that numbers are soft.
- null: no clear rule fires.

Return both a tag and a confidence (high/medium/low) based on sample sizes.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Each rule verified against a hand-constructed test place.
- Tag returned in the predict_place response.
- Tag ignores low-n circle data (< 3) to avoid false positives.

items.title: [v0.9] Tag-first place cards (casual hero view)
items.description:
CONTEXT: Ship the casual view where the tag is the hero and numbers are secondary.

GOAL: Redesign the place card so the spread tag ('💎 Hidden Gem', '⚠ Tourist Trap', '✓ Consensus') is the primary visual element, with the three tier ratings underneath in a compact row.

STYLING: tags use colored badges consistent with the existing badge classes (reuse badge-p0/p1/p2 color palette).

Keep the detail panel available for the full breakdown (peek + quant view).
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- List view renders tag-first cards.
- Detail panel still accessible.
- Null tags render cleanly (just show the tiers).

items.title: [v0.9] Quant view: numeric tiers table + derivation expansion
items.description:
CONTEXT: The advanced rendering for quant users (and curious-casual peek).

GOAL: Expand the detail panel's quant view to show:
- Full numeric tier table (rating, count, precision per tier).
- Contributors list with per-source raw rating, bias-corrected rating, similarity, precision, weight %.
- Formula derivation: Ŷ = Σ(p·r) / Σp with filled values.
- 'Spread ± ε' with a small confidence interval.

This is the main payoff of the structured predict_place response.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Quant view renders all numeric detail cleanly.
- Formula line shows actual values substituted.
- Expansion works on mobile.

items.title: [v0.9] Sort feed by spread + filter by tag
items.description:
CONTEXT: Spread-based sort/filter is a product differentiator.

GOAL: Extend the existing sort/filter row with:
- Sort: add 'Hidden Gems' (desc spread) and 'Biggest Disagreements' (abs spread).
- Filter: add tag chips (Consensus / Hidden Gem / Tourist Trap / All).
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Sort options work.
- Filter chips work.
- State persists across view mode changes.

items.title: [v0.9] 'Heads up' warning when adding a Tourist Trap to your Want-to-Try
items.description:
CONTEXT: Soft nudge, not a block.

GOAL: When a user is about to add a place tagged 'tourist-trap' to their want-to-try list, show an inline note in the modal: 'Your circle rates this {N} stars lower than Google. Still want to add it?' with 'Yes, add it' / 'Never mind' buttons.

UX: non-modal, doesn't block, just informs.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Warning appears for tourist-trap tagged places only.
- Does not appear for other tags.
- User can still add if they want.

items.title: [v0.9] Explainability footer: 'We show all three so you can read between them'
items.description:
CONTEXT: First-time user framing.

GOAL: On first visit to a place detail page after v0.9 ships (track with a flag), show a one-time tooltip/bubble explaining the three tiers: 'Google = everyone's opinion. Reviewers = critics you've tuned. Your Circle = your people.'

Dismiss with 'Got it' — never show again for this user.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- First detail-view visit shows tooltip.
- Subsequent visits do not.
- State stored per-user.

items.title: [v1.0] Onboarding flow: sign-in → find friends → rate 5 places → calibrate reviewers
items.description:
CONTEXT: The canonical new-user journey.

GOAL: Build a guided onboarding wizard triggered on first sign-in:
1. Welcome + mission statement (short).
2. Find friends — search by handle/email OR invite via link.
3. Rate 5 places you know — autocomplete via Google Places; quick-pick from a list of popular places in the user's city to reduce friction.
4. Swipe-based reviewer calibration (reuse the v0.8 game).
5. 'Your first predictions' — show 3 tagged place cards generated from initial signal.

Skippable at any step. Re-entry point in Settings.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Full flow works end-to-end for a new account.
- Each step is skippable.
- 'Rate 5 places' auto-populates entries.
- Post-onboarding, Discover feed has content.

items.title: [v1.0] Materialize similarity matrix for fast feed rendering
items.description:
CONTEXT: predict_place is called per-card on feed renders. As users and places grow, this won't scale.

GOAL: Create a materialized view or per-user cache table that precomputes the predicted rating + tag for each (user, place) pair, refreshed nightly alongside similarity_scores.

Feed reads become a single indexed lookup rather than a per-card computation.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Feed p95 render time under 300ms for a 50-card feed.
- Refresh job runs nightly.
- Stale-cache indicator present (for cards where the underlying data has changed since last refresh).

items.title: [v1.0] PWA install support + mobile polish
items.description:
CONTEXT: Inner Table feels mobile-first; make it installable.

GOAL: Add manifest.json, service worker, install prompt. Polish mobile-specific UI issues (safe-area insets, iOS rubber-band scroll, keyboard-pushes-input).

Confirm the app works offline for cached entries (read-only).
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- App is installable on iOS and Android.
- Splash/icon present.
- Offline mode shows cached entries with a banner indicating offline state.
- No mobile viewport issues on iPhone SE / iPhone 15 / Pixel 7.

items.title: [v0.6] Analytics + error tracking (PostHog or Plausible)
items.description:
CONTEXT: Can't optimize what you don't measure.

GOAL: Integrate PostHog (recommended — product analytics + session replay) or Plausible (simpler, privacy-friendly). Track:
- Onboarding funnel completion.
- Add-place, rate, comment, react events.
- Discover feed engagement (views, adds to Want-to-Try).
- Friend-invite conversion.

Also set up Sentry (or similar) for JS error tracking.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- PostHog/Plausible dashboard shows events.
- Sentry captures a deliberate test exception.
- No PII leaked to analytics beyond what's acceptable per privacy policy.

items.title: [v1.0] Production deploy pipeline + custom domain
items.description:
CONTEXT: Stop deploying by copy-paste or whatever the current mechanism is. Get a proper CI/CD.

GOAL: Set up a deploy pipeline (Vercel, Netlify, or GitHub Pages + Actions). Wire custom domain (e.g. innertable.app). Separate staging and prod Supabase projects.

On push to main, deploy to staging. On tag release, deploy to prod. Document in docs/deploy.md.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Prod and staging URLs work.
- Custom domain points to prod.
- Deploy runs on every push.
- Rollback procedure documented.

items.title: [v1.0] Launch checklist: ToS, privacy policy, rate limits, monitoring
items.description:
CONTEXT: Pre-launch hygiene.

GOAL: Produce and ship:
- Terms of Service and Privacy Policy (template-based is fine; link from footer).
- Rate limits on Supabase RPCs (especially friend invite — prevent spam).
- Uptime monitoring on prod URL.
- Backup/restore procedure for Supabase data (documented + tested).
- Incident response runbook (what to do when something breaks).

Reminder: this is a learning project launching to friends. Don't over-engineer — a Notion page for the runbook is fine.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- ToS + Privacy linked from the app footer.
- Rate limits active on key endpoints.
- Uptime monitor running (UptimeRobot / BetterStack).
- Backup tested: restore from a snapshot works.
items.affectedFiles: docs/tos.md, docs/privacy.md, docs/runbook.md, index.html (footer), supabase/config.toml (rate limits)

items.title: [v0.2] Set up Supabase project and local dev environment
items.description:
CONTEXT: Migrating InnerTable off Firebase RTDB to Supabase (Postgres + Auth). This item only sets up the Supabase side — no app code changes yet.

GOAL: Create a new Supabase project, configure local dev (supabase CLI, .env setup), and confirm we can connect from a scratch HTML page.

STEPS:
- Create a Supabase project at supabase.com (free tier).
- Install supabase CLI locally and link the project.
- Add a .env.local file (gitignored) with SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY.
- Create a docs/supabase-setup.md with setup instructions for future reference.
- Verify connection with a one-page test HTML that reads from a dummy table.

DON'T: modify index.html or existing app JS yet — this is infra-only. Do not commit the .env file. Show me the diff before committing.
items.acceptanceCriteria:
- Supabase project exists and is linked locally.
- .env.local contains working SUPABASE_URL + SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, and .env.local is gitignored.
- docs/supabase-setup.md walks through the setup end-to-end.
- A throwaway test HTML connects and reads/writes successfully.

Code Refactoring

releases.goals: Normalize the schema: places, entries, comments, reactions as first-class tables. Refactor UI to 'one place, many takes'.
